_purpose: Corpus-gravity A/B catalog. Run the SAME entries TWICE — once with gravity_classifier_enabled=false (baseline) and once =true (treatment) — then diff with scripts/gravity_ab_aggregate.py. Focused on the SILENT-intervention wells (defuse-rephrase + one-token-nudge) where gravity-on-vs-off is a clean UNATTENDED comparison. The offer-fork wells (doom/wolfenstein/quake/racing) PAUSE for a human fork tap, so they are measured via manual runs + the landing-ledger's gravityFork/webgl, NOT this unattended catalog. Per-entry extra fields (keyword/phrasing/expected_dimensionality/gravity_policy) are read by the aggregator; the wizard ignores them. Land = render-probe webgl matches expected_dimensionality (3D well → webgl true).

entries.goal: Build a solar system you can explore — the sun and the planets orbiting it.
entries.notes: 2D-diagram prior; gravity ON should silently append the explicit-3D clause → real WebGL.

entries.goal: Build a flight simulator where you fly a plane over terrain.
Build a molecule viewer that shows a 3D ball-and-stick model you can rotate.
Build a Minecraft-style world you can walk around and place blocks in.
entries.notes: gravity ON should inject the 'voxel' token → holds it at real WebGL.

entries.goal: Build a particle galaxy — thousands of stars swirling in space.
Build an interactive globe of the Earth you can spin and zoom.
Build a kart racer where you drive a go-kart around a track.

entries.notes: CONTROL: 3D-basin well, neutral phrasing already lands WebGL. Gravity inert → on==off expected.

entries.goal: Build a real 3D WebGL scene with three.js: a rotating lit cube with OrbitControls.
entries.notes: CONTROL: goal already carries the renderer token → gravity no-action → on==off expected.
NEGATIVE CONTROL: deep-2D well. Gravity must NOT push it to 3D (webgl should stay false).
NEGATIVE CONTROL: deep-2D well. Gravity inert; webgl false is correct.
